bids.reasoning: At the opening with no opponent offers or messages, I target my maximum-utility bid and avoid conceding before seeing their priorities. Since time pressure is zero, there is no need to trade away value yet, especially on the high-weight issues C and D.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still the opening phase at t=0.02, so there is little reason to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s all-valueA offer is extremely poor for my preferences, and their message about having no room to move is unsupported cheap talk this early. I will make a small concession on my lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the more important issues.

I am still targeting a very high-utility bid because it is only t=0.04 and the opponent has not yet shown any real concession, only repeated their worst-for-me package with unverifiable “principal authorization” claims. I will make a small concession on my lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the more important issues B, C, and D.

I am still targeting high utility because it is very early (t=0.06), and the opponent’s repeated valueA package gives me very low utility with no real concession despite their messages. I will continue conceding only on my lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the high-weight issues C and D and important issueB.

I am targeting a high-utility bid by conceding only on my lowest-weight issue, issueA, while holding firm on the more important issues B, C, and D. Since it is still very early at t=0.08 and the opponent has repeated the same all-valueA offer without numeric concession, I treat their authorization claims as cheap talk rather than evidence that I must accept a very low-utility package.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.82 because it is still very early, so there is no need to concede on the high-weight issues C and D yet. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueA offer and claims they cannot move, but that is cheap talk and not supported by any concession pattern, so I will only concede on my lowest-weight issueA for now.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.76 because it is still early at t=0.12, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s very low-utility package yet. The opponent’s messages claim firm limits, but their actual offers show at least slight movement on issueC, so I treat the claims as cheap talk while making a modest concession on lower-weight issueB and holding firm on high-weight issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.72 because it is still early in the negotiation and there is no need to approach the opponent’s very low offers yet. The opponent’s messages claim no flexibility, but their move from issueC:valueA to issueC:valueB shows some concession despite the “authorization” language, so I am trusting the offer history over the cheap talk. I am conceding further on lower-weight issueB while holding firm on high-weight issueC and issueD.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because the negotiation is early at t=0.16, while making a small additional concession on issueB after already conceding issueA fully. The opponent’s messages claim a hard authorization limit, but their actual offers show at least some movement on issueC, so I treat the claims as cheap talk and continue holding firm on my highest-weight issues issueC and issueD.

At t=0.18 it is still early, but the opponent has held mostly firm and only moved issueC from valueA to valueB, so I should make a meaningful but controlled concession. I am targeting a utility around 0.66 by conceding issueA and issueB to their apparent preferred valueA while holding firm on the high-importance issues issueC and issueD. Their authorization claims are cheap talk, but the repeated offers suggest they strongly want low values on issueA/issueB/issueD and have slight flexibility on issueC.

I am targeting a strong early-game utility around 0.64 while avoiding repeating my previous bid. The opponent’s messages claim immovable limits, but their offers show some concession on issueC; since it is still early at t=0.20, I will make only a modest concession while holding firm on high-value issueD.

At t=0.22 it is still early-to-mid negotiation, so I am targeting a solid positive utility while making a measured concession rather than accepting the opponent’s very low-value packages. The opponent’s messages about fixed authorization are contradicted by their actual movement on issueA and issueC, so I trust the bid history and concede mainly on lower/previously signaled areas while holding issueD, one of my highest-weight issues, at valueF.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the low 0.6 range because we are only at t=0.24, so I should not concede heavily on high-weight issues yet. The opponent’s messages claim no room to move, but their offers have in fact shifted on issueA and issueC, so I trust the bidding history rather than the authorization claims. I will concede on issueA and issueB, match their recent movement on issueC, and make only a small concession on high-weight issueD.

I am targeting a utility around 0.69 by keeping strong positions on the high-weight issues C and D while using the opponent’s latest signal that issueB: valueB is workable. At t=0.26 there is still time, so I should not concede heavily on issueD yet; their repeated “no room” messages are contradicted by actual movement on issueA, issueB, and issueC, so I rely on the offer history rather than the claims.

I am targeting a still-solid utility around the mid-0.6 range, since we are only at t=0.28 and there is no need to collapse to the opponent’s low bids yet. The opponent’s messages claim fixed authority, but their actual offers have changed across issueA, issueB, issueC, and now issueD, so I trust the bidding pattern over the cheap talk. I am conceding on lower-value/previously contested areas while holding meaningful value on issueC and issueD, which matter most to me.

I am targeting a utility around the mid-0.6 range: still well above reservation, but with meaningful concessions since we are at t=0.30. The opponent’s actual offers have improved only slightly and inconsistently despite repeated “final authority” messages, so I am trusting the numbers rather than the claims. Their latest bid moved issueB to valueD, which is nearly as good for me as valueB, so I can align there while still holding reasonable ground on high-weight issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.65 by holding the higher-weight issuesC and issueD at valueC/valueD while conceding issueA fully and using issueB valueB, which the opponent has previously offered. The opponent’s repeated “no room to move” messages are contradicted by their actual concessions over time, especially on issueC, issueB, and now issueD, so I rely on the bid history rather than the claims. At t=0.32 there is still time, so I avoid dropping to their latest low-utility package while proposing a combination of concessions they have already signaled separately.

I am targeting a moderate concession around the mid-game while preserving value on the high-weight issues, especially issueC and issueD. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim no flexibility, but their actual offers show gradual movement on issueA, issueC, and issueD, so I am combining concessions they have signaled with a still-positive bid for me. Since time is only 0.34, I do not need to accept their low package yet, but I should avoid stalling with the same offer.

I am targeting a utility around 0.64, which is a meaningful concession from my early positions while still protecting my high-weight issues C and D at this mid-stage time. The opponent’s repeated “no authority” messages are contradicted by their actual concessions over time, including movement on B, C, and D, so I treat the messages as cheap talk and respond to the bidding pattern. I concede issueA fully and align with their latest issueB value, while keeping issueC and issueD at values they have partly signaled movement toward.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.59, conceding mainly on lower-weight issueA and issueB while preserving acceptable value on the high-weight issueC and issueD. The opponent’s repeated “authorization” messages are not credible given they have continued to move in the offer history, and their bids show gradual concessions especially on issueC, with occasional movement on issueA, issueB, and issueD. Since time is 0.38, I will make a meaningful but not final concession and avoid simply repeating my immediately previous bid.

I am targeting roughly the same utility as my last offer while reshuffling concessions toward values the opponent has recently signaled, especially issueB: valueC. At t=0.40 we are in the mid-game, so I should make a meaningful but controlled concession on issueD while holding issueC at valueC because issueC and issueD are highly important to me. The opponent’s repeated “authorization limit” messages are contradicted by their changing offers, so I rely on the actual bid history rather than the claims.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.54, below my prior offer but still well above the opponent’s latest offer to me. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim no flexibility, but the offer history shows gradual concessions, especially on issueC and issueD, so I am using their latest movement while asking for a small improvement on issueB. Since time is only 0.42, I should concede meaningfully but not collapse to their exact bid yet.

I am targeting a moderate concession around the low-to-mid utility range rather than capitulating, since we are still before the deadline but the opponent’s offers have improved somewhat. Their repeated “authorization limit” messages are contradicted by their actual movement, so I trust the offer history and concede on lower/less costly points while holding issueD slightly above their latest bid. I accept their latest positions on issueA, issueB, and issueC, but ask for issueD: valueC, which they have previously offered.
